column1: # Canonical class-I tumor-associated neoantigens from the oncology literature,
# with published HLA restriction and T-cell response outcome. Used as a
# stage-5 sanity check: NetMHCpan should call these as binders (IC50 < 5000 nM)
# and the strong subset (IC50 < 100 nM per literature) as strong binders.
#   - source_type=binder: published to bind its HLA allele and, in most cases,
#     published to elicit a CD8+ T-cell response in patients.
#   - source_type=anchor_broken: the same binder with the pos-2 and C-terminal
#     anchor residues replaced by glycine. For HLA-A*02:01 these positions are
#     strongly biased toward L/I/M/V; replacing with G is the standard way to
#     produce a clean synthetic non-binder without the "curation bias" of
#     IEDB's Negative set. Used as a negative control for the binder AUC.
# Citations (PMIDs) reference the primary paper that first reported the
# peptide + HLA pairing; many of these are now canonical across the field.
